observations.recommendation:
We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unidentified balances amounting to ₱94,103.22 and make the necessary adjustment /correction, as warranted; and

Provide necessary disclosures in the NFS to enable the users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended Management to direct the BAC to assess the existing procedures in the preparation of contracts and POs as well as the submission of the same to the auditor concerned and identifying the gaps and make the necessary corrective actions to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended Management the following:

Formulation of policy relative to the acceptance of delivered items taking into consideration the delivery system to be adopted, manpower requirement and specific procedures to be followed which will serve as a unified guidelines for compliance within the LGU;

Thereafter, direct the designated supply and property custodian to notify the auditor concerned of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance thereof; and

Direct the inspection officer to prepare and furnish the auditor concerned with the copy of inspection report within 24 hours from acceptance of the items delivered.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Review the lapsing schedule and recompute PPE depreciation; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the depreciation expense, accumulated depreciation and net book values of the affected PPE accounts.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Reclassify completed projects as of December 31, 2024, under the CIP-Infrastructure Assets account totaling ₱2.016 million to Infrastructure Assets account and recognize the depreciation expense of the completed project from the time it was completed and accepted by the agency; and
Henceforth, reclassify completed projects under CIP account to the appropriate PPE accounts as soon as the project is completed, in accordance with the recognition criteria of IPSAS 17 and Section 50, Chapter 3, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the procurement of the air conditioner to the appropriate PPE account; and
Office Equipment					₱56,180.00
Other Supplies and Materials Inventory			₱48,780.00
Prior Years Adjustment				₱  7,400.00
Henceforth, adheres to the PPE recognition and measurement criteria set under IPSAS 17 for proper accounting.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management, through the:
Municipal Accountant:
Together with the various offices inventory custodian and Inventory Committee, to conduct physical inventory count of supplies and materials to establish the inventory balance on hand in the books of accounts.
RHU and WCPMH to:
Assign a designated inventory custodian responsible for tracking supplies and maintaining updated records/reports; and
Implement inventory monitoring report (WCPMH Pharmacy) and submit SSMI to Municipal Accounting Office as basis for recognition of supplies expenses.
Inventory Committee to:
Formulate guidelines and procedures on how to conduct the physical inventory to include all inventories including those that were already issued to the different end-user offices;
Set the timelines for the physical count, preparation and submission of the required reports to facilitate reconciliation; and
Conduct complete physical count on all inventories semi-annually and to submit the RPCI to Municipal Accounting Office as required by MNGAS for LGUs.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management ensure that the Accounting Section:
Coordinate with the Philhealth Office to secure updated records or confirmation as to the status of the hospital claims from January 2015 to August 2019;
Prepare appropriate adjusting entries based on the result of the verification, including the recognition of any necessary allowance for impairment and write-off denied claims;
Formulate and adopt a policy for determining the rate of allowance for impairment of receivables based on probability of collections, aging of receivables, and other relevant factors; and
Set up an allowance for impairment of receivables, as necessary, in compliance with IPSAS 29 to fairly present the valuation of receivables in the FS.

Recommendation
.
We recommended, and Management agreed to secure and consolidate supporting documentation of the dormant receivables deemed uncollectible and initiate the process of requesting for authority for their write-off, pursuant with the guidelines prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to clear the accounting records and to reflect the true status of the receivables as reported in the FS.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant, henceforth, record expenses according to the nature of the transactions involved to prevent accounts misstatements and ensure the fair presentation of the FS; and
Local Finance Committee utilize historical data during the review process to anticipate the occurrence of unforeseen expenditures, and exercise due diligence in identifying the appropriate accounts in accordance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-009 and IPSAS.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to align the preparation of the NFS with the disclosure requirements of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and of the pertinent IPSAS provisions to provide additional information to the users and readers in analyzing the statements and understanding of the contents included in the NFS.

Recommendation
We reiterated our recommendation that Management:
Reconstitute the Inventory Committee to ensure completion of the inventory-taking procedures.
Inventory Committee to:
Prepare and submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process;
Complete the physical inventory of PPE in accordance with Item 6.2 of the COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Prepare and submit the RPCPPE covering all the PPE accounts to the Property Section and Municipal Accounting Office for reconciliation.
Property Section and Municipal Accounting Office to:
Undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Section and the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Municipal Accounting Office are reconciled. The reconciliation shall be completed within 10 days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed, through the LSB to:
Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to determine the exact and accurate net available SEF balance, including total prior years’ unexpended funds; and
Revise the SEF budget preparation process to ensure that all available unexpended balances are properly accounted for and included in the succeeding budget proposals.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant strictly adheres to the content requirements of the SEF Utilization Report as prescribed in Annex B of DBM-DepEd-DOF JC No. 01, s. 2017, ensuring only allowable education-related expenditures are reported and reflecting the SEF balance available for budgeting for the following year.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management, through the GFPS, ensure strict adherence to the timelines in the submission of GAD AR, in accordance with the provisions in COA Circular No. 2014-001 and PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reclassify the claims of various officers and employees to Due to Officers and Employees account;

Conduct a thorough analysis on the items listed as “Others” and various claims of officers and employees to determine whether the same shall qualify as part of the accounts payable based on the recognition criteria for liabilities, otherwise cause the necessary adjustment; and

Present the required disclosure in the NFS by incorporating the separate schedule prepared and provide other relevant information as deem necessary to enable the users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reclassify the following items to their appropriate accounts (1) auditing services in the amount of ₱119,595.50; (2) accounting services in the amount of ₱2,860.00; (3) election reserved in the amount ₱18,563.62; (4) fund transfer from the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) Regional Field Office No. IX in the amount of ₱529,255.00; and (5) Funds for ICS Gender and Development (GAD) Program in the amount of ₱70,000.00; and

Provide a separate schedule for Due to LGUs account containing the necessary disclosures such as but not limited to nature of transaction and source agency in the NFS to enable users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended Management the following:

Direct the Officials concerned in the preparation and review of the Utilization Report of the LDRRMF to make the necessary adjustment/correction on the said report taking into consideration the corresponding actual cash in bank balance to ensure the accuracy and correctness of the report; and

Moving forward, perform a monthly reconciliation/ comparison between the cash in bank balances reflected in the Utilization Report and the corresponding BRS to ensure that both reports contain accurate data.
We recommended Management to ensure that all expenditures to be charged against the unexpended balance of LDRRMF shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption.

We recommended Management to:

Direct the LDRRM Office to ensure that programs, projects and activities (PPAs) identified during the preparation of the annual LDRRMIP shall be implementable and if possible be implemented within the budget year; and

Advise the LDRRM Council to conduct monitoring, review, and evaluation of yearly accomplishments to assess whether the overall objective of the LDRRM was achieved, including the determination of whether the project(s) under the continuing appropriations is/are still consistent with the local plans otherwise recommend for appropriate action.

We recommended Management, through the Local Finance Committee, to assess the current budget and recommend feasible measures to the Local Sanggunian to address the need for a garbage truck and reinstate the dump truck acquired using the LDRRMF to its intended usage.

We recommended Management, through the Local School Board (LSB), to:

Refrain from charging the regular Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of various schools to the SEF; and

Ensure that the priority PPAs to be included in the subsequent preparation of SEF budget shall be consistent with the objectives of the fund set forth under RA No. 7160.

We recommended Management the following:

Formulation of policy on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance taking into consideration the existing guidelines and cascading the same to all officials and employees concerned;

Consider an arrangement with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) to open and maintain a payroll account for all casual, contractual and job order employee so that payment of salaries shall be made through Authority to Debit Account (ADA), otherwise if it is not possible a single cash advance shall be drawn for the payment of salaries of casual, contractual and job order employee regardless of their station; and

Conduct assessment on cash advances granted for operating activities to determine whether there is a need to designate additional special disbursing officer specially on overlapping schedule of activities

We recommended Management to direct the BAC to adopt corrective measures to address the gaps observed particularly on procurements through alternative methods, ensuring that the agency fully abides with the standards set forth under RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unidentified balances amounting to ₱94,103.22 and make the necessary adjustment / correction or disclosures, if warranted; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared in the notes to the financial statements and provide other relevant information as deemed necessary.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the following items to its appropriate accounts (1) fund transfer from the DSWD Regional Field Office No. IX in the amounting of ₱529,255.00; (2) auditing services in the amount of ₱119,595.50; and (3) election reserved in the amount ₱18,563.62; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared for Due to LGUs as part of the necessary disclosures in the notes to the financial statement and supply other relevant information that will enable users of the financial statement to understand composition of the said accounts such as but not limited to outstanding cash balance and the status of implementation of the related project program.

Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Conduct a thorough analysis on the items listed as “Others” and various claims of officers and employees to determine whether the same shall qualify as part of the accounts payable based on the recognition criteria for liabilities, otherwise cause the necessary adjustment; and
Present the required disclosure in the NFS by incorporating the separate schedule prepared and provide other relevant information as deem necessary to enable the users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the following items to their appropriate accounts (1) auditing services in the amount of ₱119,595.50; (2) accounting services in the amount of ₱2,860.00; (3) election reserved in the amount ₱18,563.62; (4) fund transfer from the DSWD Regional Field Office No. IX in the amount of ₱529,255.00; and (5) Funds for ICS GAD Program in the amount of ₱70,000.00; and
Provide a separate schedule for Due to LGUs account containing the necessary disclosures such as but not limited to nature of transaction and source agency in the NFS to enable users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unidentified balances amounting to ₱94,103.22 and make the necessary adjustment /correction, as warranted; and
Provide necessary disclosures in the NFS to enable the users to understand the nature of the account.

We recommended and Management agreed the following:
Direct the Officials concerned in the preparation and review of the Utilization Report of the LDRRM Fund to make the necessary adjustment/correction on the said report taking into consideration the corresponding actual cash in bank balance to ensure the accuracy and correctness of the report; and
Moving forward, perform a monthly reconciliation/comparison between the cash in bank balances reflected in the Utilization Report and the corresponding Bank Reconciliation Statement to ensure that both reports contain accurate data.
Moving forward, we recommended and Management to ensure that all expenditures to be charge against the unexpended balance of LDRRMF shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption.

Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the LDRRM Office to ensure that PPAs identified during the preparation of annual LDRRMIP shall be implementable and if possible be implemented within the budget year; and
Advise the LDRRMC to conduct monitoring, review, and evaluation of yearly accomplishments to assess whether the overall objective of the LDRRM was achieved, including the determination of whether the project(s) under the continuing appropriations is/are still consistent with the local plans otherwise recommend for appropriate action.

We recommended and Management agreed through the Local Finance Committee to assess the current budget and recommend feasible measures to the Local Sanggunian to address the need for a garbage truck and reinstate the dump truck acquired using the LDRRMF to its intended usage.

We recommended and management agreed through the LSB to:
Refrain from charging the regular MOOE of various schools to the Special Education Fund; and
Ensure that the priority programs, projects, and activities to be included in the subsequent preparation of SEF budget shall be consistent with the objectives of the fund set forth under R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Formulation of policy on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance taking into consideration the existing guidelines and cascading the same to all officials and employees concerned;
Consider an arrangement with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) to open and maintain a payroll account for all casual, contractual and job order employee so that payment of salaries shall be made through Authority to Debit Account (ADA), otherwise if it is not possible a single cash advance shall be drawn for the payment of salaries of casual, contractual and job order employee regardless of their station; and
Conduct assessment on cash advances granted for operating activities to determine whether there is a need to designate additional special disbursing officer specially on overlapping schedule of activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to adopt corrective measures to address the gaps observed particularly on procurements through alternative methods, ensuring that the agency fully abides with the standards set forth under RA 9184 and its RIRR.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the BAC to assess the existing procedures in the preparation of contracts and POs as well as the submission of the same to the auditor concerned and identifying the gaps and make the necessary corrective actions to comply with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Formulation of policy relative to the acceptance of delivered items taking into consideration the delivery system to be adopted, manpower requirement and specific procedures to be followed which will serve as a unified guidelines for compliance within the LGU;
Thereafter, direct the designated supply and property custodian to notify the auditor concerned of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance thereof; and
Direct the inspection officer, to prepare and furnish the auditor concerned with the copy of inspection report within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of the items delivered.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer together with the Property/Supply Officer to strictly observe the submission of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) supported by Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS); and

Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the required periodic physical inventory of supplies each semester ending June 30 and December 31 and submit the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of Inventories to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the existence of the unidentified balances as of December 31, 2019 and make the necessary adjustment / correction or disclosures, if warranted;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared in the noted to the financial statements and provide other relevant information needed; and

Moving forward, the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the entries made in general journal shall reflect the true nature of events that transpired for a particular account.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to incorporate the separate schedule prepared and provide other necessary disclosure that will enable users of the financial statement to understand the existence of such account such as but not limited to nature and purpose of the trust receipts and the status of its corresponding cash balance.
We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to Prepare the corresponding adjustment to correct the erroneous entry pertaining to the outright recording of expenses on purchases made.

We recommended management the following:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the timeline for the submission of reports and financial transaction documents to the Accounting Office to facilitate the timely recording of the same;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the reports and financial transaction documents covering the affected period/months immediately; and

The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary action plan to address the delay in the submission of the same in the subsequent period.

We recommended management the following:

Establishment of hiring policies/guidelines and criteria (designation, function, qualification, specific/ lump sum/intermittent work/job and services, etc.);

Evaluate the necessity of 72-hour work shift for rescuers and establish work shifting of 24-hour work shift instead for continuous service delivery on a daily basis; and

Evaluate the staffing pattern of the municipality to fill up the vacant and funded positions of the LGU in order to limit the hiring of contract orders and job order employees and be guided by the pertinent provisions of the Civil Service Commission.

The Municipal Treasurer to expedite the processing of the documents needed to support the receipt of the donated property;           The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize the transfer of assets from the PLGU Sibugay in the books of accounts of the LGU; and            Further, depreciate retrospectively the PPE from the moment of receipt of the assets and not during recording of the assets.

The Municipal Treasurer to conduct the physical inventory of the Biological Asset;           The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the account to the proper asset account and to reconcile with the records of the Municipal Treasurer; and           Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Biological Assets transactions are to be in accordance with Sections 304 to 315.

The Municipal Accountant, in recording procurement made by the municipality, consider the asset recognition principle where an asset should be recognized when it is probable that future economic benefit will flow to the enterprise or agency and it can be measured reliably;           The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to properly classify the accounts to come up with reliable financial statements;           On the other hand, recording of expenditures shall only be made upon issuance of supplies and materials as provided in Section 121 of the NGAS Manual; and           Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for recording of inventories as described in sections 175-207 and the provisions for recording of Property, Plant and Equipment as described in sections 208 – 271.

The Municipal Accountant to evaluate the liabilities of the Municipality and determine the causes of the unreconciled amounts; Further, identify these liabilities and classify them to their respective accounts, while derecognizing the non-existing liabilities;           Follow the basic accounting principle on the recognition of liabilities and make the necessary correcting entries.

The Municipal Accountant to reverse the adjusting entries made and to properly reconcile the accounts by tracing and analyzing the true reasons for the non-reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts; and           Further, all Journal Entry Vouchers must be supported with valid supporting documents.

The Head of the Agency create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members to be able complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The committee shall also have at least one member each from the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units; The Inventory Committee conduct inventory taking activities according to previously approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count, using the prescribed format under the Accounting Manual and submit the same not later than January 31 of the ensuing; Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs volume II to follow the prescribed forms and reports described in sections 61 to 74 as illustrated in Appendices 54 to 67 of the manual. And to submit the same in accordance with GAM for LGUs volume I, section 266 not later than January 31 of the ensuing year;           The Property Unit and Accounting Unit undertake collaborative procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly recorded in their respective records and that the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the Property Unit and the PPE Ledger Cards (PPELCs) maintained by the Accounting Unit are reconciled; Moving forward to the implementation of the GAM for LGU follow the pertinent rules as described in volume 1, section 269; and           The management undertake disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs and derecognition procedures of non-existing/missing PPE without available record of accountability as provided for in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

Direct the completion of the conduct of the annual physical count of PPEs and Inventories and come up with the RPCPPE and RPCI and accordingly prepare the PIF to be submitted to the Audit Team and GSIS to secure insurance.

The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Local Road Network Property Card, Local Road Network Ledger Card, Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and the Report on Local Road Network; and           The Municipal Accountant to maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at year end and to properly depreciate the account; and to properly disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

The municipal assessor to communicate with the Office of the Provincial Assessor to initiate the province wide assessment and to plan and implement the expected update of the Assessment Rolls as to the conditions of the real properties, its actual use, and the exemptions on RPT, if any, and to regularly conduct general revision of assessment of real property every three (3) years in accordance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.

Institutionalization of GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered age- and sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation; Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues; and           Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.
The LGU to establish a GAD monitoring and evaluation system as required under Sections 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive insure the insurable properties of the LGU with the GSIS. The Municipal treasurer to prepare the lists of insurable properties to facilitate the processing of insurance with GSIS.

The Local Finance Committee to identify additional sources of income and/or push for the passage of updated revenue code to boost local taxation and design a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue collection based on the current state of the Municipality’s economic enterprises; and           The Municipal Treasurer to monitor, intensify and strategize collection efforts from taxpayers such as deployment of demand letters and tapping the assistance of the barangays for real property collections.
The Local Chief Executive improve the internal control system of the LGU by addressing the gaps presented.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough analysis on the unidentified debit and credit memos to determine the appropriate adjusting entry and to prepare the corresponding journal voucher thereafter; and
moving forward, direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all reconciling items identified during the preparation of bank reconciliation statement shall be adjusted on a timely manner.

We recommended management to direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the Office of the Internal Audit Services to conduct a thorough analysis on the unutilized petty cash fund and make the necessary action.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize the receipt of various assets from the provincial government in the books of accounts of the LGU; and
prepare the necessary journal entry to record the depreciation expense and restate the affected financial statements to earliest possible time.

We recommended management to:
direct the Municipal Agriculturist to furnish the Office of the Municipal Accountant with document/s evidencing the distribution/dispersal of livestock to various beneficiaries; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary entry to derecognized the Biological Assets for the books of accounts based on the documents presented by the Office of the Municipal Agriculturist.

We recommended management to come up with an action plan in accordance with the guidelines and specific procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 availing the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the overstatement of the affected accounts and
direct the Municipal Accountant to restate the financial statements for CY 2021 for comparative presentation.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts pertaining to the fund transfer from the Department of Tourism; and
prepare the necessary journal entry to record the fund transfer from the Department of Agriculture.

We recommended management to:
direct the Municipal Accountant to reverse the adjusting entries made without supporting documents; and
make a thorough analysis to determine the reason(s) for the unreconciled balance between the reciprocal accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entry if warranted.

We recommended management the following:
cause awareness by directing all collectors to strictly adhere with the provision of Sections 29 and 31(a), Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs that at the close of each business day to prepare RCD and turn over the cash collections to the liquidating officer;
direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the provision of Section 32, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs to deposit intact all collections daily or not later than the next banking day; and
direct the Municipal Treasurer to forward the RCDs together with the duplicate copy of accountable forms and validated deposit slips to the accounting unit for the preparation of journal voucher.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere with the procedures set forth in Section 44, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs that the Report of Check Issued shall be prepared and submitted daily to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of preparing the journal voucher based on the Accountant’s Advice and adhere with the proper procedure that the basis for the recording is the individual vouchers.

We recommended management to direct the Municipal Treasurer:
to accomplish the Property Inventory Form (PIF) upon completion of the RPCPPE and RPCI by the Inventory Committee and submitted the same to the Audit Team; and
to submit the Property Inventory Form (PIF) to Government Insurance Fund (GIF) of the GSIS as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage over all insurable assets and properties of the LGU and apply for the insurance policy.

We recommended management the following:
direct the Inventory Committee to include the Local Road Network in the conduct of physical count of PPEs and accomplish the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network;
 thereafter, direct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report on Local Road Network as well as the maintenance of Local Road Network Property Card; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Local Road Network Ledger Card and provide the required disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
Management’s Comment
Management agreed to comply with the recommendations.

We recommended management the following:
make representation with the Office of the Provincial Assessor to undertake general revision of assessments and property classification which has been long overdue; and
make representation with the Office of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan through the Secretary to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan to pass a resolution relative to the general revision of assessments and property classification.

We recommended management the following:
strengthen the GAD Focal Point System by capacitating the GAD Focal Person thru attendance to seminar on GAD related topics; and
thereafter, direct the GAD Focal Person to establish a comprehensive GAD database that will serve as basis in planning, programming and policy formulation for gender responsive programs and activities

We recommended management to create GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team through issuance of Executive Order or Administrative Order in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to assess the effectiveness and efficiency of GAD mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended management to direct the Local Finance Committee to come up with an action plan identifying possible additional sources of income that will augment local revenues to uplift IRA dependency of the LGU.
We recommended management to improve the internal control system by addressing the gaps presented in the previous sections.

The municipal accountant to reclassify the said PPEs as Investment Properties in accordance with IPSAS 16; and           Moving forward to the implementation of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs refer to Sections 287 to 303 for the proper recording and reporting of Investment Properties.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements; The Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 2 and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and           Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the proforma illustrations for the Notes to the Financial Statements.

The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference between the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the amount in the Statement of Financial Performance; and           Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, to strictly follow the provisions for SCBAA as described in sections 370 – 373 of Volume I.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements; and           The Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS 42 and provide required disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS 14 and provide required disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

The Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements; and           Moving forward to the issuance of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to strictly follow required disclosures for PPE as described in section 270 of Volume I.

guidelines and procedures provided under Administrative Order No.  2006-0039
to ensure the availability and relative  affordability  of  essential  drugs
and medicines through a consignment system.

Recommendation

      We recommend  that  the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  or  Consignment
Committee ensure  that  the  prevailing  market  prices  are  considered  in
evaluating the consignment offers instead of limiting the evaluation to  the
prices offered  by  the  prospective  consignors.  Likewise,  the  committee
should  also  strictly  observe  and  comply  the  specific  guidelines  and
procedures on the acquisition of drugs  and  medicines  through  consignment
prescribed under the said DOH Administrative Order.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the BAC, thru its Secretariat to comply the  Posting
requirement of Notice of Award; Notice to Proceed and Approved  Contract  in
the PhilGEPS and LGU’s website as required in  Section  37  of  RA  9184  to
enhance transparency in the Award of Contracts.
      Further, the Municipal  Mayor;  BAC;  Municipal  Engineer  and  others
concerned to submit the perfected contracts  together  with  the  supporting
documents  within  five  (5)  days  after  signing  thereof  for  Legal  and
Technical Review thereof.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the Municipal  Mayor,  MDRRMO  and  members  of  the
Sangguniang Bayan to look into this concern  of  providing  basic  necessary
amenities for an effective and workable DRF when confronted  with  emergency
events.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the LGU conduct more  information  campaign  towards
proper segregation and  collection  and  disposal  of  solid  waste  in  the
Municipality up to barangay  and  household  level  and  provide  sufficient
logistics to afford effective implementation of the program.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the LGU provide sufficient  number  of  MRF  in  the
vicinity of the LGU and at  barangay  and  household  level  to  effectively
implement the segregation of solid waste. Moreover,  the  LGU  shall  assist
the barangay by collecting non-compostable and recyclable waste to  properly
dispose thereof.

Recommendation

      We recommend that the Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan work out to  convert
the LGU’s open dump into controlled dump  to  avoid  health  hazard  to  the
nearby residents to avoid penal sanctions and for proper, safe and  sanitary
management of solid waste generated in the Municipality.
